title: 1 Chronicles (Codex Amiatinus)
source: Codex Amiatinus (c. 700 AD) folios via Internet Archive + Latin Vulgate (bolls.life) + Douay-Rheims Bible
description: The complete book of 1 Chronicles from Codex Amiatinus, the oldest surviving complete Latin Vulgate Bible, made at Wearmouth-Jarrow in Northumbria around 700 AD and now held at the Biblioteca Medicea Laurenziana in Florence (Amiatino 1). Manuscript folios from the public-domain Internet Archive facsimile (pages 689–749). Latin Vulgate text verbatim from bolls.life. English translation verbatim from Douay-Rheims Bible.
introduction:
This entry presents the entire book of 1 Chronicles from Codex Amiatinus (c. 700 AD)—the oldest surviving complete manuscript of Jerome's Latin Vulgate, made in Northumbria and now in Florence. It meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library:

  1. Manuscript scans: Sample folios from Codex Amiatinus via the public-domain Internet Archive facsimile (archive.org/details/codex-amiatinua, CC0).
  2. Original text: The complete Latin Vulgate text of 1 Chronicles, verbatim from the public-domain bolls.life API. The Vulgate is Jerome's translation (late 4th–early 5th c.), the textual tradition that Codex Amiatinus preserves.
  3. English translation: Douay-Rheims Bible, verbatim from the public-domain bolls.life API.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Adam, Seth, Enos, <sup>2</sup>Cainan, Malaleel, Jared, <sup>3</sup>Henoch, Mathusale, Lamech, <sup>4</sup>Noë, Sem, Cham, et Japtheth. <sup>5</sup>Filii Japheth : Gomer, et Magog, et Madai, et Javan, Thubal, Mosoch, Thiras. <sup>6</sup>Porro filii Gomer : Ascenez, et Riphath, et Thogorma. <sup>7</sup>Filii autem Javan : Elisa et Tharsis, Cethim et Dodanim. <sup>8</sup>Filii Cham : Chus, et Mesraim, et Phut, et Chanaan. <sup>9</sup>Filii autem Chus : Saba, et Hevila, Sabatha, et Regma, et Sabathacha. Porro filii Regma : Saba, et Dadan. <sup>10</sup>Chus autem genuit Nemrod : iste cœpit esse potens in terra.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>Adam, Seth, Enos, <sup>2</sup>Cainan, Malaleel, Jared, <sup>3</sup>Henoc, Mathusale, Lamech, <sup>4</sup>Noe, Sem, Cham, and Japheth. <sup>5</sup>The sons of Japheth: Gomer, and Magog, and Madai, and Javan, Thubal, Mosoch, Thiras. <sup>6</sup>And the sons of Gomer: Ascenez, and Riphath, and Thogorma. <sup>7</sup>And the sons of Javan: Elisa and Tharsis, Cethim and Dodanim. <sup>8</sup>The sons of Cham: Chus, and Mesrai, and Phut, and Chaanan. <sup>9</sup>And the sons of Chus: Saba, and Hevila, Sabatha, and Regma, and Sabathaca. And the sons of Regma: Saba, and Dadan. <sup>10</sup>Now Chus begot Nemrod: he began to be mighty upon earth.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>Mesraim vero genuit Ludim, et Anamim, et Laabim, et Nephtuim, <sup>12</sup>Phetrusim quoque, et Casluim : de quibus egressi sunt Philisthiim, et Caphtorim. <sup>13</sup>Chanaan vero genuit Sidonem primogenitum suum, Hethæum quoque, <sup>14</sup>et Jebusæum, et Amorrhæum, et Gergesæum, <sup>15</sup>Hevæumque et Aracæum, et Sinæum. <sup>16</sup>Aradium quoque, et Samaræum, et Hamathæum. <sup>17</sup>Filii Sem : Ælam, et Assur, et Arphaxad, et Lud, et Aram, et Hus, et Hul, et Gether, et Mosoch. <sup>18</sup>Arphaxad autem genuit Sale, qui et ipse genuit Heber. <sup>19</sup>Porro Heber nati sunt duo filii : nomen uni Phaleg, quia in diebus ejus divisa est terra ; et nomen fratris ejus Jectan. <sup>20</sup>Jectan autem genuit Elmodad, et Saleph, et Asarmoth, et Jare,
translation.text: <sup>11</sup>But Mesraim begot Ludim, and Anamim, and Laabim, and Nephtuim, <sup>12</sup>Phetrusim also, and Casluim: from whom came the Philistines, and Caphtorim. <sup>13</sup>And Chanaan beget Sidon his firstborn, and the Hethite, <sup>14</sup>And the Jebusite, and the Amorrhite, and the Gergesite, <sup>15</sup>And the Hevite, and the Aracite, and the Sinite, <sup>16</sup>And the Aradian, and the Samarite, and the Hamathite. <sup>17</sup>The sons of Sem: Elam and Asur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram, and Hus, and Hul, and Gether, and Mosoch. <sup>18</sup>And Arphaxad beget Sale, and Sale beget Heber. <sup>19</sup>And to Heber were born two sons, the name of the one was Phaleg, because In his days the earth was divided; and the name of his brother was Jectan. <sup>20</sup>And Jectan beget Elmodad, and Saleph, and Asarmoth, and Jare,

translation.original_text: <sup>21</sup>Adoram quoque, et Huzal, et Decla, <sup>22</sup>Hebal etiam, et Abimaël, et Saba, necnon <sup>23</sup>et Ophir, et Hevila, et Jobab : omnes isti filii Jectan. <sup>24</sup>Sem, Arphaxad, Sale, <sup>25</sup>Heber, Phaleg, Ragau, <sup>26</sup>Serug, Nachor, Thare, <sup>27</sup>Abram : iste est Abraham. <sup>28</sup>Filii autem Abraham, Isaac et Ismahel. <sup>29</sup>Et hæ generationes eorum. Primogenitus Ismahelis, Nabaioth, et Cedar, et Adbeel, et Mabsam, <sup>30</sup>et Masma, et Duma, Massa, Hadad, et Thema,
translation.text: <sup>21</sup>And Adoram, and Usal, and Decla, <sup>22</sup>And Hebal, and Abimael, and Saba, <sup>23</sup>And Ophir, and Hevila, and Jobab. All these are the sons of Jectan. <sup>24</sup>Sem, Arphaxad, Sale, <sup>25</sup>Heber, Phaleg, Ragau, <sup>26</sup>Serug, Nachor, Thare, <sup>27</sup>Abram, this is Abraham. <sup>28</sup>And the sons of Abraham, Isaac and Ismahel. <sup>29</sup>And these are the generations of them. The firstborn of Ismahel, Nabajoth, then Cedar, and Adbeel, and Mabsam, <sup>30</sup>And Masma, and Duma, Massa, Hadad, and Thema,

translation.original_text: <sup>31</sup>Jetur, Naphis, Cedma : hi sunt filii Ismahelis. <sup>32</sup>Filii autem Ceturæ concubinæ Abraham, quos genuit : Zamran, Jecsan, Madan, Madian, Jesboc, et Sue. Porro filii Jecsan : Saba, et Dadan. Filii autem Dadan : Assurim, et Latussim, et Laomim. <sup>33</sup>Filii autem Madian : Epha, et Epher, et Henoch, et Abida, et Eldaa : omnes hi filii Ceturæ. <sup>34</sup>Genuit autem Abraham Isaac : cujus fuerunt filii Esau, et Israël. <sup>35</sup>Filii Esau : Eliphaz, Rahuel, Jehus, Ihelom, et Core. <sup>36</sup>Filii Eliphaz : Theman, Omar, Sephi, Gathan, Cenez, Thamna, Amalec. <sup>37</sup>Filii Rahuel : Nahath, Zara, Samma, Meza. <sup>38</sup>Filii Seir : Lotan, Sobal, Sebeon, Ana, Dison, Eser, Disan. <sup>39</sup>Filii Lotan : Hori, Homam. Soror autem Lotan fuit Thamna. <sup>40</sup>Filii Sobal : Alian, et Manahath, et Ebal, Sephi et Onam. Filii Sebeon : Aja et Ana. Filii Ana : Dison.
translation.text: <sup>31</sup>Jetur, Naphis, Cedma: these are the sons of Ismahel. <sup>32</sup>And the sons of Cetura, Abraham's concubine, whom she bore: Zamran, Jecsan, Madan, Madian, Jesboc, and Sue. And the sons of Jecsan, Saba, and Dadan. And the sons of Dadan: Assurim, and Latussim, and Laomin. <sup>33</sup>And the sons of Madian: Epha, and Epher, and Henoch, and Abida, and Eldaa. All these are the sons of Cetura. <sup>34</sup>And Abraham beget Isaac: and his sons were Esau and Israel. <sup>35</sup>The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Rahuel, Jehus, Ihelom, and Core. <sup>36</sup>The sons of Eliphaz: Theman, Omar, Sephi, Gathan, Cenez, and by Thamna, Amalec. <sup>37</sup>The sons of Rahuel: Nahath, Zara, Samma, Meza. <sup>38</sup>The sons of Seir: Lotan. Sobal, Sebeen, Ana, Dison, Eser, Disan. <sup>39</sup>The sons of Lotan: Hori, Homam. And the sister of Lotan was Thamna. <sup>40</sup>The sons of Sobal: Alian, and Manahath, and Ebal, Sephi and Onam. The sons of Sebeon: Aia, and Ana. The son of Ana: Dison.

translation.original_text: <sup>41</sup>Filii Dison : Hamram, et Heseban, et Jethran, et Charan. <sup>42</sup>Filii Eser : Balaan, et Zavan, et Jacan. Filii Disan : Hus et Aran. <sup>43</sup>Isti sunt reges qui imperaverunt in terra Edom, antequam esset rex super filios Israël. Bale filius Beor : et nomen civitatis ejus, Denaba. <sup>44</sup>Mortuus est autem Bale, et regnavit pro eo Jobab filius Zare de Bosra. <sup>45</sup>Cumque et Jobab fuisset mortuus, regnavit pro eo Husam de terra Themanorum. <sup>46</sup>Obiit quoque et Husam, et regnavit pro eo Adad filius Badad, qui percussit Madian in terra Moab : et nomen civitatis ejus Avith. <sup>47</sup>Cumque et Adad fuisset mortuus, regnavit pro eo Semla de Masreca. <sup>48</sup>Sed et Semla mortuus est, et regnavit pro eo Saul de Rohoboth, quæ juxta amnem sita est. <sup>49</sup>Mortuo quoque Saul, regnavit pro eo Balanan filius Achobor. <sup>50</sup>Sed et hic mortuus est, et regnavit pro eo Adad : cujus urbis nomen fuit Phau, et appellata est uxor ejus Meetabel filia Matred filiæ Mezaab.
translation.text: <sup>41</sup>The sons of Dison: Hamram, and Eseban, and Jethran, and Charan. <sup>42</sup>The sons of Eser: Balaan, and Zavan, and Jacan. The sons of Disan: Hus and Aran. <sup>43</sup>Now these are the kings that reigned in the land of Edom, before there was a king over the children of Israel: Bale the son of Beer: and the name of his city was Denaba. <sup>44</sup>And Bale died, and Jobab the son of Zare of Bosra, reigned in his stead. <sup>45</sup>And when Jobab also was dead, Husam of the land of the Themanites reigned in his stead. <sup>46</sup>And Husam also died, and Adad the son of Badad reigned in his stead, and he defeated the Madianites in the land of Moab: and the name of his city was Avith. <sup>47</sup>And when Adad also was dead, Semla of Masreca reigned in his stead. <sup>48</sup>Semla also died, and Saul of Rohoboth, which is near the river, reigned in his stead. <sup>49</sup>And when Saul was dead, Balanan the son of Achobor reigned in his stead. <sup>50</sup>He also died, and Adad reigned in his stead: and the name of his city was Phau, and his wife was called Meetabel the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezaab.

translation.original_text: <sup>51</sup>Adad autem mortuo, duces pro regibus in Edom esse cœperunt : dux Thamna, dux Alva, dux Jetheth, <sup>52</sup>dux Oolibama, dux Ela, dux Phinon, <sup>53</sup>dux Cenez, dux Theman, dux Mabsar, <sup>54</sup>dux Magdiel, dux Hiram : hi duces Edom.
translation.text: <sup>51</sup>And after the death of Adad, there began to be dukes in Edom instead of kings: duke Thamna, duke Alva, duke Jetheth, <sup>52</sup>Duke Oolibama, duke Ela, duke Phinon, <sup>53</sup>,53Duke Cenez, duke Theman, duke Mabsar, <sup>54</sup>Duke Magdiel, duke Hiram. These are the dukes of Edom.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Filii autem Israël : Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Juda, Issachar, et Zabulon, <sup>2</sup>Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Nephthali, Gad, et Aser. <sup>3</sup>Filii Juda : Her, Onan, et Sela : hi tres nati sunt ei de filia Sue Chananitide. Fuit autem Her primogenitus Juda malus coram Domino, et occidit eum. <sup>4</sup>Thamar autem nurus ejus peperit ei Phares et Zara : omnes ergo filii Juda, quinque. <sup>5</sup>Filii autem Phares : Hesron et Hamul. <sup>6</sup>Filii quoque Zaræ : Zamri, et Ethan, et Eman, Chalchal quoque, et Dara, simul quinque. <sup>7</sup>Filii Charmi : Achar, qui turbavit Israël, et peccavit in furto anathematis. <sup>8</sup>Filii Ethan : Azarias. <sup>9</sup>Filii autem Hesron qui nati sunt ei : Jerameel, et Ram, et Calubi. <sup>10</sup>Porro Ram genuit Aminadab. Aminadab autem genuit Nahasson, principem filiorum Juda.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>And these are the sons of Israel: Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Juda, Issachar, and Zabulon, <sup>2</sup>Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Nephtali, Gad, and Aser. <sup>3</sup>The sons of Juda: Her, Onan and Sela. These three were born to him of the Chanaanitess the daughter of Sue. And Her the firstborn of Juda, was wicked in the sight of the Lord, and he slew him. <sup>4</sup>And Thamar his daughter in law bore him Phares and Zara. So all the sons of Juda were five. <sup>5</sup>And the sons of Phares, were Hesron and Hamul. <sup>6</sup>And the sons also of Zare: Zamri, and Ethan, and Eman, and Chalchal, and Dara, five in all. <sup>7</sup>And the sons of a Charmi: Achar, who troubled Israel, and sinned by the theft of the anathema. <sup>8</sup>The sons of Ethan: Azarias, <sup>9</sup>And the sons of Hesron that were born to him: Jerameel, and Ram, and Calubi. <sup>10</sup>And Ram beget Aminadab, and Aminadab beget Nahasson, prince of the children of Juda.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>Nahasson quoque genuit Salma, de quo ortus est Booz. <sup>12</sup>Booz vero genuit Obed, qui et ipse genuit Isai. <sup>13</sup>Isai autem genuit primogenitum Eliab, secundum Abinadab, tertium Simmaa, <sup>14</sup>quartum Nathanaël, quintum Raddai, <sup>15</sup>sextum Asom, septimum David. <sup>16</sup>Quorum sorores fuerunt Sarvia et Abigail. Filii Sarviæ : Abisai, Joab, et Asaël, tres. <sup>17</sup>Abigail autem genuit Amasa, cujus pater fuit Jether Ismahelites. <sup>18</sup>Caleb vero filius Hesron accepit uxorem nomine Azuba, de qua genuit Jerioth : fueruntque filii ejus Jaser, et Sobab, et Ardon. <sup>19</sup>Cumque mortua fuisset Azuba, accepit uxorem Caleb Ephratha, quæ peperit ei Hur. <sup>20</sup>Porro Hur genuit Uri, et Uri genuit Bezeleel.
translation.text: <sup>11</sup>And Nahasson beget Salma, the father of Boot. <sup>12</sup>And Boot beget Obed, and Obed beget Isai. <sup>13</sup>And Isai beget Eliab his firstborn, the second Abinadab, the third Simmaa, <sup>14</sup>The fourth, Nathanael, the fifth Raddai, <sup>15</sup>The sixth Asom, the seventh David. <sup>16</sup>And their sisters were Sarvia, and Abigail. The sons of Sarvia: Abisai, Joab, and Asael, three. <sup>17</sup>And Abigail bore Amasa, whose father was Jether the Ismahelite. <sup>18</sup>And Caleb the son of Hesron took a wife named Azuba, of whom he had Jerioth: and her sons were Jaser, and Sobab, and Ardon. <sup>19</sup>And when Azuba was dead, Caleb took to wife Ephrata: who bore him Hur. <sup>20</sup>And Hur beget Uri: and Uri beget Bezeleel.

translation.original_text: <sup>21</sup>Post hæc ingressus est Hesron ad filiam Machir patris Galaad, et accepit eam cum esset annorum sexaginta : quæ peperit ei Segub. <sup>22</sup>Sed et Segub genuit Jair, et possedit viginti tres civitates in terra Galaad. <sup>23</sup>Cepitque Gessur et Aram oppida Jair, et Canath, et viculos ejus sexaginta civitatum : omnes isti filii Machir patris Galaad. <sup>24</sup>Cum autem mortuus esset Hesron, ingressus est Caleb ad Ephratha. Habuit quoque Hesron uxorem Abia, quæ peperit ei Assur patrem Thecuæ. <sup>25</sup>Nati sunt autem filii Jerameel primogeniti Hesron, Ram primogenitus ejus, et Buna, et Aram, et Asom, et Achia. <sup>26</sup>Duxit quoque uxorem alteram Jerameel, nomine Atara, quæ fuit mater Onam. <sup>27</sup>Sed et filii Ram primogeniti Jerameel fuerunt Moos, Jamin, et Achar. <sup>28</sup>Onam autem habuit filios Semei et Jada. Filii autem Semei : Nadab et Abisur. <sup>29</sup>Nomen vero uxoris Abisur, Abihail, quæ peperit ei Ahobban et Molid. <sup>30</sup>Filii autem Nadab fuerunt Saled et Apphaim. Mortuus est autem Saled absque liberis.
translation.text: <sup>21</sup>And afterwards Hesron went in to the daughter of Machir the father of Galaad, and took her to wife when he was threescore years old: and she bore him Segub. <sup>22</sup>And Segub beget Jair, and he had three and twenty cities in the land of Galaad. <sup>23</sup>And he took Gessur, and Aram the towns of Jair, and Canath, and the villages thereof, threescore cities. All these, the sons of Machir father of Galaad. <sup>24</sup>And when Hesron was dead, Caleb went in to Ephrata. Hesron also had to wife Abia who bore him Ashur the father of Thecua. <sup>25</sup>And the sons of Jerameel the firstborn of Hesron, were Ram his firstborn, and Buna, and Aram, and Asom, and Achia. <sup>26</sup>And Jerameel married another wife, named Atara, who was the mother of Onam. <sup>27</sup>And the sons of Ram the firstborn of Jerameel, were Moos, Jamin, and Achar. <sup>28</sup>And Onam had sons Semei, and Jada. And the sons of Semei: Nadab, and Abisur. <sup>29</sup>And the name of Abisur's wife was Abihail, who bore him Ahobban, and Molid. <sup>30</sup>And the sons of Nadab were Saled, and Apphaim. And Saled died without children.

translation.original_text: <sup>31</sup>Filius vero Apphaim, Jesi : qui Jesi genuit Sesan. Porro Sesan genuit Oholai. <sup>32</sup>Filii autem Jada fratris Semei : Jether, et Jonathan. Sed et Jether mortuus est absque liberis. <sup>33</sup>Porro Jonathan genuit Phaleth, et Ziza. Isti fuerunt filii Jerameel. <sup>34</sup>Sesan autem non habuit filios, sed filias : et servum ægyptium nomine Jeraa. <sup>35</sup>Deditque ei filiam suam uxorem : quæ peperit ei Ethei. <sup>36</sup>Ethei autem genuit Nathan, et Nathan genuit Zabad. <sup>37</sup>Zabad quoque genuit Ophlal, et Ophlal genuit Obed. <sup>38</sup>Obed genuit Jehu, Jehu genuit Azariam, <sup>39</sup>Azarias genuit Helles, et Helles genuit Elasa. <sup>40</sup>Elasa genuit Sisamoi, Sisamoi genuit Sellum,
translation.text: <sup>31</sup>But the son of Apphaim was Jesi: and Jesi beget Sesan. And Sesan beget Oholai. <sup>32</sup>And the sons of Jada the brother of Semei: Jether and Jonathan. And Jether also died without children. <sup>33</sup>But Jonathan beget Phaleth, and Ziza, These were the sons of Jerameel. <sup>34</sup>And Sesan had no sons, but daughters and a servant an Egyptian, named Jeraa. <sup>35</sup>And he gave him his daughter to wife: and she bore him Ethei. <sup>36</sup>And Ethei begot Nathan, and Nathan beget Zabad. <sup>37</sup>And Zabad beget Ophlal, and Ophlal beget Obed. <sup>38</sup>Obed beget Jehu, Jehu beget Azarias. <sup>39</sup>Azarias beget Helles, and Helles begot Elasa. <sup>40</sup>Elasa beget Sisamoi, Sisamoi beget Sellum,

translation.original_text: <sup>41</sup>Sellum genuit Icamiam, Icamia autem genuit Elisama. <sup>42</sup>Filii autem Caleb fratris Jerameel : Mesa primogenitus ejus ; ipse est pater Ziph : et filii Maresa patris Hebron. <sup>43</sup>Porro filii Hebron, Core, et Taphua, et Recem, et Samma. <sup>44</sup>Samma autem genuit Raham, patrem Jercaam, et Recem genuit Sammai. <sup>45</sup>Filius Sammai, Maon : et Maon pater Bethsur. <sup>46</sup>Epha autem concubina Caleb peperit Haran, et Mosa, et Gezez. Porro Haran genuit Gezez. <sup>47</sup>Filii autem Jahaddai, Regom, et Joathan, et Gesan, et Phalet, et Epha, et Saaph. <sup>48</sup>Concubina Caleb Maacha, peperit Saber, et Tharana. <sup>49</sup>Genuit autem Saaph pater Madmena Sue, patrem Machbena et patrem Gabaa. Filia vero Caleb fuit Achsa. <sup>50</sup>Hi erant filii Caleb, filii Hur primogeniti Ephratha, Sobal pater Cariathiarim.
translation.text: <sup>41</sup>Sellum beget Icamia, and Icamia begot Elisama. <sup>42</sup>Now the sons of Caleb the brother of Jerameel were Mesa his firstborn, who was the father of Siph: and the sons of Maresa father of Hebron. <sup>43</sup>And the sons of Hebron, Core, and Thaphua, and Recem, and Samma. <sup>44</sup>And Samma beget Raham, the father of Jercaam, and Recem beget Sammai. <sup>45</sup>The son of Sammai, Maon: and Maon the father of Bethsur. <sup>46</sup>And Epha the concubine of Caleb bore Haran, and Mesa, and Gezez. And Haran beget Gezez. <sup>47</sup>And the sons of Jahaddai, Rogom, and Joathan, and Gesan, and Phalet, and Epha, and Saaph. <sup>48</sup>And Maacha the concubine of Caleb bore Saber, and Tharana. <sup>49</sup>And Saaph the father of Madmena beget Sue the father of Machbena, and the father of Gabaa. And the daughter of Caleb was Achsa. <sup>50</sup>These were the sons of Caleb, the son of Hur the firstborn of Ephrata, Sobal the father of Cariathiarim.

translation.original_text: <sup>51</sup>Salma pater Bethlehem, Hariph pater Bethgader. <sup>52</sup>Fuerunt autem filii Sobal patris Cariathiarim, qui videbat dimidium requietionum. <sup>53</sup>Et de cognatione Cariathiarim, Jethrei, et Aphuthei, et Semathei, et Maserei. Ex his egressi sunt Saraitæ, et Esthaolitæ. <sup>54</sup>Filii Salma, Bethlehem, et Netophathi, coronæ domus Joab, et dimidium requietionis Sarai : <sup>55</sup>cognationes quoque scribarum habitantium in Jabes, canentes atque resonantes, et in tabernaculis commorantes. Hi sunt Cinæi, qui venerunt de Calore patris domus Rechab.
translation.text: <sup>51</sup>Salma the father of Bethlehem, Hariph the father of Bethgader. <sup>52</sup>And Sobal the father of Cariathiarim had sons: he that saw half of the places of rest. <sup>53</sup>And of the kindred of Cariathiarim, the Jethrites, and Aphuthites, and Semathites, and Maserites. Of them came the Saraites, and Esthaolites. <sup>54</sup>The sons of Salma, Bethlehem, and Netophathi, the crowns of the house of Joab, and half of the place of rest of Sarai. <sup>55</sup>And the families of the scribes that dwell in Jabes, singing and making melody, and abiding in tents. These are the Cinites, who came of Calor (Chamath) father of the house of Rechab,

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>David vero hos habuit filios, qui ei nati sunt in Hebron : primogenitum Amnon ex Achinoam Jezrahelitide, secundum Daniel de Abigail Carmelitide, <sup>2</sup>tertium Absalom filium Maacha filiæ Tholmai regis Gessur, quartum Adoniam filium Aggith, <sup>3</sup>quintum Saphathiam ex Abital, sextum Jethraham de Egla uxore sua. <sup>4</sup>Sex ergo nati sunt ei in Hebron, ubi regnavit septem annis et sex mensibus. Triginta autem et tribus annis regnavit in Jerusalem. <sup>5</sup>Porro in Jerusalem nati sunt ei filii, Simmaa, et Sobab, et Nathan, et Salomon, quatuor de Bethsabee filia Ammiel : <sup>6</sup>Jebaar quoque et Elisama, <sup>7</sup>et Eliphaleth, et Noge, et Nepheg, et Japhia, <sup>8</sup>necnon Elisama, et Eliada, et Elipheleth, novem :
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>Now these were the sons of David that were born to him in Hebron: the firstborn Amnon of Achinoam the Jezrahelitess, the second Daniel of Abigail the Carmelitess. <sup>2</sup>The third Absalom the son of Maacha the daughter of Tolmai king of Gessur, the fourth Adonias the son of Aggith, <sup>3</sup>The fifth Saphatias of Abital, the sixth Jethrahem of Egla, his wife. <sup>4</sup>So six sons were born to him in Hebron, where he reigned seven years and six months. And in Jerusalem he reigned three and thirty years. <sup>5</sup>And these sons were born to him in Jerusalem: Simmaa, and Sobab, and Nathan, and Solomon, four of Bethsabee the daughter of Ammiel. <sup>6</sup>Jebaar also and Elisama, <sup>7</sup>And Eliphaleeh, and Noge, and Nepheg, and Japhia, <sup>8</sup>And Elisama, and Eliada, and Elipheleth, nine:

translation.original_text: <sup>9</sup>omnes hi, filii David absque filiis concubinarum : habueruntque sororem Thamar. <sup>10</sup>Filius autem Salomonis, Roboam : cujus Abia filius genuit Asa. De hoc quoque natus est Josaphat, <sup>11</sup>pater Joram : qui Joram genuit Ochoziam, ex quo ortus est Joas : <sup>12</sup>et hujus Amasias filius genuit Azariam. Porro Azariæ filius Joatham <sup>13</sup>procreavit Achaz patrem Ezechiæ, de quo natus est Manasses. <sup>14</sup>Sed et Manasses genuit Amon patrem Josiæ. <sup>15</sup>Filii autem Josiæ fuerunt : primogenitus Johanan, secundus Joakim, tertius Sedecias, quartus Sellum. <sup>16</sup>De Joakim natus est Jechonias, et Sedecias.
translation.text: <sup>9</sup>All these the sons of David, beside the sons of the concubines: and they had a sister Thamar. <sup>10</sup>And Solomon's son was Roboam: whose son Abia beget Asa. And his son was Josaphat, <sup>11</sup>The father of Joram: and Joram be- got Ochozias, of whom was born Joas: <sup>12</sup>And his son Amasias begot Azarias. And Joathan the son of Azarias <sup>13</sup>Beget Achaz, the father of Ezechias, of whom was born Manasses. <sup>14</sup>And Manasses beget Amen the father of Josias. <sup>15</sup>And the sons of Josias were, the firstborn Johanan, the second Joakim, the third Sedecias, the fourth Sellum. <sup>16</sup>Of Joakim was born Jechonias, and Sedecias.

translation.original_text: <sup>17</sup>Filii Jechoniæ fuerunt : Asir, Salathiel, <sup>18</sup>Melchiram, Phadaia, Senneser, et Jecemia, Sama, et Nadabia. <sup>19</sup>De Phadaia orti sunt Zorobabel et Semei. Zorobabel genuit Mosollam, Hananiam, et Salomith sororem eorum : <sup>20</sup>Hasaban quoque, et Ohol, et Barachian, et Hasadian, Josabhesed, quinque. <sup>21</sup>Filius autem Hananiæ, Phaltias pater Jeseiæ, cujus filius Raphaia : hujus quoque filius, Arnan, de quo natus est Obdia, cujus filius fuit Sechenias. <sup>22</sup>Filius Secheniæ, Semeia : cujus filii Hattus, et Jegaal, et Baria, et Naaria, et Saphat, sex numero. <sup>23</sup>Filius Naariæ, Elioënai, et Ezechias, et Ezricam, tres. <sup>24</sup>Filii Elioënai, Oduia, et Eliasub, et Pheleia, et Accub, et Johanan, et Dalaia, et Anani, septem.
translation.text: <sup>17</sup>The sons of Jechonias were Asir, Salathiel, <sup>18</sup>Melchiram, Phadaia, Senneser and Jecemia, Sama, and Nadabia. <sup>19</sup>Of Phadaia were born Zorobabel and Semei. Zorobabel beget Mosollam, Hananias, and Salomith their sister: <sup>20</sup>Hasaba also, and Ohol, and Barachias, and Hasadias, Josabhesed, five. <sup>21</sup>And the son of Hananias was Phaltias the father of Jeseias, whose son was Raphaia. And his son was Arnan, of whom was born Obdia, whose son was Sechenias. <sup>22</sup>The son of Sechenias, was Semeia, whose sons were Hattus, and Jegaal, and Baria, and Naaria, and Saphat, six in number. <sup>23</sup>The sons of Naaria, Elioenai, and Ezechias, and Ezricam, three. <sup>24</sup>The sons of Elioenai, Oduia, and Eliasub, and Pheleia, and Accub, and Johanan, and Dalaia, and Anani, seven.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Filii Juda : Phares, Hesron, et Charmi, et Hur, et Sobal. <sup>2</sup>Raia vero filius Sobal genuit Jahath, de quo nati sunt Ahumai, et Laad : hæ cognationes Sarathi. <sup>3</sup>Ista quoque stirps Etam : Jezrahel, et Jesema, et Jedebos. Nomen quoque sororis eorum, Asalelphuni. <sup>4</sup>Phanuel autem pater Gedor, et Ezer pater Hosa : isti sunt filii Hur primogeniti Ephratha patris Bethlehem. <sup>5</sup>Assur vero patri Thecuæ erant duæ uxores, Halaa et Naara. <sup>6</sup>Peperit autem ei Naara, Oozam, et Hepher, et Themani, et Ahasthari : isti sunt filii Naara. <sup>7</sup>Porro filii Halaa, Sereth, Isaar et Ethnan. <sup>8</sup>Cos autem genuit Anob, et Soboba, et cognationem Aharehel filii Arum. <sup>9</sup>Fuit autem Jabes inclytus præ fratribus suis, et mater ejus vocavit nomen illius Jabes, dicens : Quia peperi eum in dolore. <sup>10</sup>Invocavit vero Jabes Deum Israël, dicens : Si benedicens benedixeris mihi, et dilataveris terminos meos, et fuerit manus tua mecum, et feceris me a malitia non opprimi. Et præstitit Deus quæ precatus est.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>The sons of Juda: Phares, Hesron, and Charmi, and Hur, and Sobal. <sup>2</sup>And Raia the son of Sobal beget Jahath, of whom were born Ahumai, and Laad. These are the families of Sarathi. <sup>3</sup>And this is the posterity of Etam: Jezrahel, and Jesema, and Jedebos: and the name of their sister was Asalelphuni. <sup>4</sup>And Phanuel the father of Gedor, and Ezar the father of Hosa, these are the sons of Hur the firstborn of Ephratha the father of Bethlehem. <sup>5</sup>And Assur the father of Thecua had two wives, Halaa and Naara: <sup>6</sup>And Naara bore him Ozam, and Hepher, and Themani, and Ahasthari: these are the sons of Naara. <sup>7</sup>And the sons of Halaa, Sereth, Isaar, and Ethnan. <sup>8</sup>And Cos begot Anob, and Soboba, the kindred of Aharehel the son of Arum. <sup>9</sup>And Jabes was more honourable than any of his brethren, and his mother called his name Jabes, saying: Because I bore him with sorrow. <sup>10</sup>And Jabes called upon the God of Israel, saying: If blessing thou wilt bless me, and wilt enlarge my borders, and thy hand be with me, and thou save me from being oppressed by evil. And God granted him the things he prayed for.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>Caleb autem frater Sua genuit Mahir, qui fuit pater Esthon. <sup>12</sup>Porro Esthon genuit Bethrapha, et Phesse, et Tehinna patrem urbis Naas : hi sunt viri Recha. <sup>13</sup>Filii autem Cenez, Othoniel, et Saraia. Porro filii Othoniel, Hathath, et Maonathi. <sup>14</sup>Maonathi genuit Ophra, Saraia autem genuit Joab patrem Vallis artificum : ibi quippe artifices erant. <sup>15</sup>Filii vero Caleb filii Jephone, Hir, et Ela, et Naham. Filii quoque Ela : Cenez. <sup>16</sup>Filii quoque Jaleleel : Ziph, et Zipha, Thiria, et Asraël. <sup>17</sup>Et filii Ezra, Jether, et Mered, et Epher, et Jalon, genuitque Mariam, et Sammai, et Jesba patrem Esthamo. <sup>18</sup>Uxor quoque ejus Judaia, peperit Jared patrem Gedor, et Heber patrem Socho, et Icuthiel patrem Zanoë : hi autem filii Bethiæ filiæ Pharaonis, quam accepit Mered. <sup>19</sup>Et filii uxoris Odaiæ sororis Naham patris Ceila, Garmi, et Esthamo, qui fuit de Machathi. <sup>20</sup>Filii quoque Simon, Amnon, et Rinna filius Hanan, et Thilon. Et filii Jesi, Zoheth, et Benzoheth.
translation.text: <sup>11</sup>And Caleb the brother of Sua beget Mahir, who was the father of Esthon. <sup>12</sup>And Esthon beget Bethrapha, and Phesse, and Tehinna father of the city of Naas: these are the men of Recha. <sup>13</sup>And the sons of Cenez were Othoniel,. and Saraia. And the sons of Othoniel, Hathath, and Maonathi. <sup>14</sup>Maonathi beget Ophra, and Saraia begot Joab the father of the Valley of artificers: for artificers were there. <sup>15</sup>And the sons of Caleb the son of Jephone, were Hir, and Ela, and Naham. And the sons of Ela: Cenez. <sup>16</sup>The sons also of Jaleleel: Ziph, and Zipha, Thiria, and Asrael. <sup>17</sup>And the sons of Esra, Jether, and Mered, and Epher, and Jalon, and he beget Mariam, and Sammai, and Jesba the father of Esthamo. <sup>18</sup>And his wife Judaia, bore Jared the father of Gedor, and Heber the father of Socho, and Icuthiel the father of Zanoe. And these are the sons of Bethia the daughter of Pharao, whom Mered took to wife. <sup>19</sup>And the sons of his wife Odaia the sister of Naham the father of Celia, Garmi, and Esthamo, who was of Machathi. <sup>20</sup>The sons also of Simon, Amnon, and Rinna the son of Hanan, and Thilon. And the sons of Jesi Zoheth, and Benzoheth.

translation.original_text: <sup>21</sup>Filii Sela, filii Juda : Her pater Lecha, et Laada pater Maresa, et cognationes domus operantium byssum in domo juramenti. <sup>22</sup>Et qui stare fecit solem, virique Mendacii, et Securus, et Incendens, qui principes fuerunt in Moab, et qui reversi sunt in Lahem : hæc autem verba vetera. <sup>23</sup>Hi sunt figuli habitantes in plantationibus et in sepibus, apud regem in operibus ejus : commoratique sunt ibi. <sup>24</sup>Filii Simeon : Namuel et Jamin, Jarib, Zara, Saul. <sup>25</sup>Sellum filius ejus, Mapsam filius ejus, Masma filius ejus. <sup>26</sup>Filii Masma : Hamuel filius ejus, Zachur filius ejus, Semei filius ejus. <sup>27</sup>Filii Semei sedecim, et filiæ sex : fratres autem ejus non habuerunt filios multos, et universa cognatio non potuit adæquare summam filiorum Juda. <sup>28</sup>Habitaverunt autem in Bersabee, et Molada, et Hasarsuhal, <sup>29</sup>et in Bala, et in Asom, et in Tholad, <sup>30</sup>et in Bathuel, et in Horma, et in Siceleg,
translation.text: <sup>21</sup>The sons of h Sela the son of Juda: Her the father of Lecha, and Laada the father of Maresa, and the families of the house of them that wrought fine linen in the House of oath. <sup>22</sup>And he that made the sun to stand, and the men of Lying, and Secure, and Burning, who were princes in Moab, and who returned into Lahem. Now these are things of old. <sup>23</sup>These are the potters, and they dwelt in Plantations, and Hedges, with the king for his works, and they abode there. <sup>24</sup>The sons of Simeon: Namuel, and Jamin, Jarib, Zara, Saul: <sup>25</sup>Sellum his son, Mapsam his son, Masma his son. <sup>26</sup>The sons of Masma: Hamuel his son, Zachur his son, Semei his son. <sup>27</sup>The sons of Semei were sixteen, and six daughters: but his brethren had not many sons, and the whole kindred could not reach to the sum of the children of Juda. <sup>28</sup>And they dwelt in Bersabee, and Molada, and Hasarsuhal, <sup>29</sup>And in Bala, and in Asom, and in Tholad, <sup>30</sup>And in Bathuel, and in Horma, and in Siceleg,

translation.original_text: <sup>31</sup>et in Bethmarchaboth, et in Hasarsusim, et in Bethberai, et in Saarim : hæ civitates eorum usque ad regem David. <sup>32</sup>Villæ quoque eorum : Etam, et Aën, Remmon, et Thochen, et Asan, civitates quinque. <sup>33</sup>Et universi viculi eorum per circuitum civitatum istarum usque ad Baal : hæc est habitatio eorum, et sedium distributio. <sup>34</sup>Mosobab quoque et Jemlech, et Josa filius Amasiæ, <sup>35</sup>et Joël, et Jehu filius Josabiæ filii Saraiæ filii Asiel, <sup>36</sup>et Elioënai, et Jacoba, et Isuhaia, et Asaia, et Adiel, et Ismiel, et Banaia, <sup>37</sup>Ziza quoque filius Sephei filii Allon filii Idaia filii Semri filii Samaia. <sup>38</sup>Isti sunt nominati principes in cognationibus suis, et in domo affinitatum suarum multiplicati sunt vehementer. <sup>39</sup>Et profecti sunt ut ingrederentur in Gador usque ad orientem vallis, et ut quærerent pascua gregibus suis. <sup>40</sup>Inveneruntque pascuas uberes, et valde bonas, et terram latissimam et quietam et fertilem, in qua ante habitaverant de stirpe Cham.
translation.text: <sup>31</sup>And in Bethmarchaboth, and in Hasarsusim, and in Bethberai, and in Saarim. These were their cities unto the reign of David. <sup>32</sup>Their towns also were Etam, and Aen, Remmon, and Thochen, and Asan, five cities. <sup>33</sup>And all their villages round about these cities as far as Baal. This was their habitation, and the distribution of their dwellings. <sup>34</sup>And Mosabab and Jemlech, and Josa, the son of Amasias, <sup>35</sup>And Joel, and Jehu the son of Josabia the son of Saraia, the son of Asiel, <sup>36</sup>And Elioenai, and Jacoba, and Isuhaia, and Asaia, and Adiel, and Ismiel, and Banaia, <sup>37</sup>Ziza also the son of Sephei the son of Allon the son of Idaia the son of Semri the son of Samaia. <sup>38</sup>These were named princes in their kindreds, and in the houses of their families were multiplied exceedingly. <sup>39</sup>And they went forth to enter into Gador as far as to the east side of the valley, to seek pastures for their flocks. <sup>40</sup>And they found fat pastures, and very good, and a country spacious, and quiet, and fruitful, in which some of the race of Cham had dwelt before.

translation.original_text: <sup>41</sup>Hi ergo venerunt, quos supra descripsimus nominatim, in diebus Ezechiæ regis Juda : et percusserunt tabernacula eorum, et habitatores qui inventi fuerant ibi, et deleverunt eos usque in præsentem diem : habitaveruntque pro eis, quoniam uberrimas pascuas ibidem repererunt. <sup>42</sup>De filiis quoque Simeon abierunt in montem Seir viri quingenti, habentes principes Phalthiam et Naariam et Raphaiam et Oziel filios Jesi : <sup>43</sup>et percusserunt reliquias, quæ evadere potuerant, Amalecitarum, et habitaverunt ibi pro eis usque ad diem hanc.
translation.text: <sup>41</sup>And these whose names are written above, came in the days of Ezechias king of Juda: and they beat down their tents, and slew the inhabitants that were found there, and utterly destroyed them unto this day: and they dwelt in their place, because they found there fat pastures. <sup>42</sup>Some also of the children of Simeon, five hundred men, went into mount Seir, having for their captains Phaltias and Naaria and Raphaia and Oziel the sons of Jesi: <sup>43</sup>And they slew the remnant of the Amalecites, who had been able to escape, and they dwelt there in their stead unto this day.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Filii quoque Ruben primogeniti Israël. (Ipse quippe fuit primogenitus ejus : sed cum violasset thorum patris sui, data sunt primogenita ejus filiis Joseph filii Israël, et non est ille reputatus in primogenitum. <sup>2</sup>Porro Judas, qui erat fortissimus inter fratres suos, de stirpe ejus principes germinati sunt : primogenita autem reputata sunt Joseph.) <sup>3</sup>Filii ergo Ruben primogeniti Israël : Enoch, et Phallu, Esron, et Carmi. <sup>4</sup>Filii Joël : Samia filius ejus, Gog filius ejus, Semei filius ejus, <sup>5</sup>Micha filius ejus, Reia filius ejus, Baal filius ejus, <sup>6</sup>Beera filius ejus, quem captivum duxit Thelgathphalnasar rex Assyriorum, et fuit princeps in tribu Ruben. <sup>7</sup>Fratres autem ejus, et universa cognatio ejus, quando numerabantur per familias suas, habuerunt principes Jehiel, et Zachariam. <sup>8</sup>Porro Bala filius Azaz filii Samma filii Joël, ipse habitavit in Aroër usque ad Nebo, et Beelmeon.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>Now the sons of Ruben the firstborn of Israel, (for he was his firstborn: but forasmuch as he defiled his father's bed, his first birthright was given to the sons of Joseph the son of Israel, and he was not accounted for the firstborn. <sup>2</sup>But of the race of Juda, who was the strongest among his brethren, came the princes: but the first birthright was accounted to Joseph.) <sup>3</sup>The sons then of Ruben the firstborn of Israel were Enoch, and Phallu, Esron, and Charmi. <sup>4</sup>The sons of Joel: Samaia his son, Gog his son, Semei his son, <sup>5</sup>Micha his son, Reia his son, Baal his son, <sup>6</sup>Beera his son, whom Thelgathphalnasar king of the Assyrians carried away captive, and he was prince in the tribe of Ruben. <sup>7</sup>And his brethren, and all his kindred, when they were numbered by their families, had for princes Jehiel, and Zacharias. <sup>8</sup>And Bala the son of Azaz, the son of Samma, the son of Joel, dwelt in Aroer as far as Nebo, and Beelmeon.

translation.original_text: <sup>9</sup>Contra orientalem quoque plagam habitavit usque ad introitum eremi, et flumen Euphraten. Multum quippe jumentorum numerum possidebant in terra Galaad. <sup>10</sup>In diebus autem Saul præliati sunt contra Agareos, et interfecerunt illos, habitaveruntque pro eis in tabernaculis eorum, in omni plaga quæ respicit ad orientem Galaad. <sup>11</sup>Filii vero Gad e regione eorum habitaverunt in terra Basan usque Selcha : <sup>12</sup>Joël in capite, et Saphan secundus : Janai autem et Saphat in Basan. <sup>13</sup>Fratres vero eorum secundum domos cognationum suarum, Michaël, et Mosollam, et Sebe, et Jorai, et Jachan, et Zie, et Heber, septem. <sup>14</sup>Hi filii Abihail, filii Huri, filii Jara, filii Galaad, filii Michaël, filii Jesesi, filii Jeddo, filii Buz. <sup>15</sup>Fratres quoque, filii Abdiel filii Guni, princeps domus in familiis suis. <sup>16</sup>Et habitaverunt in Galaad, et in Basan, et in viculis ejus, et in cunctis suburbanis Saron, usque ad terminos.
translation.text: <sup>9</sup>And eastward he had his habitation as far as the entrance of the desert, and the river Euphrates. For they possessed a great number of cattle in the land of Galaad. <sup>10</sup>And in the days of Saul they fought against the Agarites, and slew them, and dwelt in their tents in their stead, in all the country, that looketh to the east of Galaad. <sup>11</sup>And the children of Gad dwelt over against them in the land of Basan, as far as Selcha: <sup>12</sup>Johel the chief, and Saphan the second: and Janai, and Saphat in Basan. <sup>13</sup>And their brethren according to the houses of their kindreds, were Michael, and Mosollam, and Sebe, and Jorai, and Jacan, and Zie, and Heber, seven. <sup>14</sup>These were the sons of Abihail, the son of Hurl, the son of Jara, the son of Galaad, the son of Michael, the son of Jesisi, the son of Jeddo, the son of But. <sup>15</sup>And their brethren the sons of Abdiel, the son of Guni, chief of the house in their families, <sup>16</sup>And they dwelt in Galaad, and in Basan and in the towns thereof, and in all the suburbs of Saron, unto the borders.

translation.original_text: <sup>17</sup>Omnes hi numerati sunt in diebus Joathan regis Juda, et in diebus Jeroboam regis Israël. <sup>18</sup>Filii Ruben, et Gad, et dimidiæ tribus Manasse, viri bellatores, scuta portantes et gladios, et tendentes arcum, eruditique ad prælia, quadraginta quatuor millia et septingenti sexaginta, procedentes ad pugnam. <sup>19</sup>Dimicaverunt contra Agareos : Ituræi vero, et Naphis, et Nodab <sup>20</sup>præbuerunt eis auxilium. Traditique sunt in manus eorum Agarei, et universi qui fuerant cum eis, quia Deum invocaverunt cum præliarentur : et exaudivit eos, eo quod credidissent in eum. <sup>21</sup>Ceperuntque omnia quæ possederant, camelorum quinquaginta millia, et ovium ducenta quinquaginta millia, et asinos duo millia, et animas hominum centum millia. <sup>22</sup>Vulnerati autem multi corruerunt : fuit enim bellum Domini. Habitaveruntque pro eis usque ad transmigrationem. <sup>23</sup>Filii quoque dimidiæ tribus Manasse possederunt terram a finibus Basan usque Baal, Hermon, et Sanir, et montem Hermon : ingens quippe numerus erat. <sup>24</sup>Et hi fuerunt principes domus cognationis eorum : Epher, et Jesi, et Eliel, et Ezriel, et Jeremia, et Odoia, et Jediel, viri fortissimi et potentes, et nominati duces in familiis suis.
translation.text: <sup>17</sup>All these were numbered in the days of Joathan king of Juda, and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel. <sup>18</sup>The sons of Ruben, and of Gad, and of the half tribe of Manasses, fighting men, bearing shields, and swords, and bending the bow, and trained up to battles, four and forty thousand seven hundred and threescore that went out to war. <sup>19</sup>They fought against the Agarites: but the Itureans, and Naphis, and Nodab, <sup>20</sup>Gave them help. And the Agarites were delivered into their hands, and all that were with them, because they called upon God in the battle: and he heard them, because they had put their faith in him. <sup>21</sup>And they took all that they possessed, of camels fifty thousand, and of sheep two hundred and fifty thousand, and of asses two thousand, and of men a hundred thousand souls. <sup>22</sup>And many fell down slain: for it was the battle of the Lord. And they dwelt in their stead till the captivity. <sup>23</sup>And the children of the half tribe of Manasses possessed the land, from the borders of Basan unto Baal, Hermon, and Sanir, and mount Hermon, for their number was great. <sup>24</sup>And these were the heads of the house of their kindred, Epher, and Jesi, and Eliel, and Esriel, and Jeremia, and Odoia, and Jediel, most valiant and powerful men, and famous chiefs in their families.

translation.original_text: <sup>25</sup>Reliquerunt autem Deum patrum suorum, et fornicati sunt post deos populorum terræ, quos abstulit Deus coram eis. <sup>26</sup>Et suscitavit Deus Israël spiritum Phul regis Assyriorum, et spiritum Thelgathphalnasar regis Assur : et transtulit Ruben, et Gad, et dimidiam tribum Manasse, et adduxit eos in Lahela, et in Habor, et Ara, et fluvium Gozan, usque ad diem hanc.
translation.text: <sup>25</sup>But they forsook the God of their fathers, and went astray after the gods of the people of the land, whom God destroyed before them. <sup>26</sup>And the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Phul king of the Assyrians, and the spirit of Thelgathphalnasar king of Assur: and he carried away Ruben, and Gad, and the half tribe of Manasses, and brought them to Lahela, and to Habor, and to Ara, and to the river of Gozan, unto this day.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Filii Levi : Gerson, Caath, et Merari. <sup>2</sup>Filii Caath : Amram, Isaar, Hebron, et Oziel. <sup>3</sup>Filii Amram : Aaron, Moyses, et Maria. Filii Aaron : Nadab et Abiu, Eleazar, et Ithamar. <sup>4</sup>Eleazar genuit Phinees, et Phinees genuit Abisue. <sup>5</sup>Abisue vero genuit Bocci, et Bocci genuit Ozi. <sup>6</sup>Ozi genuit Zaraiam, et Zaraias genuit Meraioth. <sup>7</sup>Porro Meraioth genuit Amariam, et Amarias genuit Achitob. <sup>8</sup>Achitob genuit Sadoc, et Sadoc genuit Achimaas, <sup>9</sup>Achimaas genuit Azariam, Azarias genuit Johanan, <sup>10</sup>Johanan genuit Azariam : ipse est qui sacerdotio functus est in domo quam ædificavit Salomon in Jerusalem.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>The sons of Levi were Gerson, Caath, and Merari. <sup>2</sup>The sons of Caath: Amram, Isaar, Hebron, and Oziel. <sup>3</sup>The children of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Mary. The sons of Aaron: Nadab and Abiu, Eleazar and Ithamar. <sup>4</sup>Eleazar beget Phinees, and Phinees beget Abisue, <sup>5</sup>And Abisue beget Bocci, and Bocci begot Ozi. <sup>6</sup>Ozi beget Zaraias, and Zaraias beget Maraioth. <sup>7</sup>And Maraioth beget Amarias, and Amarias beget Achitob. <sup>8</sup>Achitob beget Sadoc, and Sadoc begot Achimaas. <sup>9</sup>Achimaas beget Azarias, Azarias begot Johanan, <sup>10</sup>Johanan beget Azarias. This is he that executed the priestly office in the house which Solomon built in Jerusalem.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>Genuit autem Azarias Amariam, et Amarias genuit Achitob, <sup>12</sup>Achitob genuit Sadoc, et Sadoc genuit Sellum, <sup>13</sup>Sellum genuit Helciam, et Helcias genuit Azariam, <sup>14</sup>Azarias genuit Saraiam, et Saraias genuit Josedec. <sup>15</sup>Porro Josedec egressus est, quando transtulit Dominus Judam et Jerusalem per manus Nabuchodonosor. <sup>16</sup>Filii ergo Levi : Gersom, Caath, et Merari. <sup>17</sup>Et hæc nomina filiorum Gersom : Lobni, et Semei. <sup>18</sup>Filii Caath : Amram, et Isaar, et Hebron, et Oziel. <sup>19</sup>Filii Merari : Moholi et Musi. Hæ autem cognationes Levi secundum familias eorum. <sup>20</sup>Gersom : Lobni filius ejus, Jahath filius ejus, Zamma filius ejus,
translation.text: <sup>11</sup>And Azarias beget Amarias, and Amarias beget Achitob. <sup>12</sup>And Achitob beget Sadoc, and Sadoc beget Sellum, <sup>13</sup>Sellum beget Helcias, and Helcias beget Azarias, <sup>14</sup>Azarias beget Saraias, and Saraias beget Josedec. <sup>15</sup>Now Josedec went out, when the Lord carried away Juda, and Jerusalem, by the hands of Nabuchodonosor. <sup>16</sup>So the sons of Levi were Gerson, Caath, and Merari. <sup>17</sup>And these are the names of the sons of Gerson: Lobni and Semei. <sup>18</sup>The sons of Caath: Amram, and Isaar, and Hebron, and Oziel. <sup>19</sup>The sons of Merari: Moholi and Musi. And these are the kindreds of Levi according to their families. <sup>20</sup>Of Gerson: Lobni his son, Jahath his son, Zamma his son,

translation.original_text: <sup>21</sup>Joah filius ejus, Addo filius ejus, Zara filius ejus, Jethrai filius ejus. <sup>22</sup>Filii Caath : Aminadab filius ejus, Core filius ejus, Asir filius ejus, <sup>23</sup>Elcana filius ejus, Abiasaph filius ejus, Asir filius ejus, <sup>24</sup>Thahath filius ejus, Uriel filius ejus, Ozias filius ejus, Saul filius ejus. <sup>25</sup>Filii Elcana, Amasai et Achimoth <sup>26</sup>et Elcana. Filii Elcana : Sophai filius ejus, Nahath filius ejus, <sup>27</sup>Eliab filius ejus, Jeroham filius ejus, Elcana filius ejus. <sup>28</sup>Filii Samuel : primogenitus Vasseni, et Abia. <sup>29</sup>Filii autem Merari, Moholi : Lobni filius ejus, Semei filius ejus, Oza filius ejus, <sup>30</sup>Sammaa filius ejus, Haggia filius ejus, Asaia filius ejus.
translation.text: <sup>21</sup>Joah his son, Addo his son, Zara his son, Jethrai his son. <sup>22</sup>The sons of Caath, Aminadab his son, Core his son, Asir his son, <sup>23</sup>Elcana his son, Abiasaph his son, Asir his son, <sup>24</sup>Thahath his son, Uriel his son, Ozias his son, Saul his son. <sup>25</sup>The sons of Elcana: Amasai, and Achimoth. <sup>26</sup>And Elcana. The sons of Elcana: Sophai his son, Nahath his son, <sup>27</sup>Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, Elcana his son. <sup>28</sup>The sons of Samuel: the firstborn Vasseni, and Abia. <sup>29</sup>And the sons of Merari, Moholi: Lobni his son, Semei his son, Oza his son, <sup>30</sup>Sammaa his son, Haggia his son, Asaia his son.

translation.original_text: <sup>31</sup>Isti sunt quos constituit David super cantores domus Domini, ex quo collocata est arca : <sup>32</sup>et ministrabant coram tabernaculo testimonii, canentes donec ædificaret Salomon domum Domini in Jerusalem : stabant autem juxta ordinem suum in ministerio. <sup>33</sup>Hi vero sunt qui assistebant cum filiis suis, de filiis Caath, Hemam cantor filius Johel, filii Samuel, <sup>34</sup>filii Elcana, filii Jeroham, filii Eliel, filii Thohu, <sup>35</sup>filii Suph, filii Elcana, filii Mahath, filii Amasai, <sup>36</sup>filii Elcana, filii Johel, filii Azariæ, filii Sophoniæ, <sup>37</sup>filii Thahath, filii Asir, filii Abiasaph, filii Core, <sup>38</sup>filii Isaar, filii Caath, filii Levi, filii Israël. <sup>39</sup>Et frater ejus Asaph, qui stabat a dextris ejus, Asaph filius Barachiæ, filii Samaa, <sup>40</sup>filii Michaël, filii Basaiæ, filii Melchiæ,
translation.text: <sup>31</sup>These are they, whom David set over the singing men of the house of the Lord, after that the p ark was placed: <sup>32</sup>And they ministered before the tabernacle of the testimony, with singing, until Solomon built the house of the Lord in Jerusalem, and they stood according to their order in the ministry. <sup>33</sup>And these are they that stood with their sons, of the sons of Caath, Hemam a singer, the son of Joel, the son of Sammuel, <sup>34</sup>The son of Elcana, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Thohu, <sup>35</sup>The son of Suph, the son of Elcana, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai, <sup>36</sup>The son of Elcana, the son of Johel, the son of Azarias, the son of Sophonias, <sup>37</sup>The son of Thahath, the son of Asir, the son or Abiasaph, the son of Core, <sup>38</sup>The son of Isaar, the son of Caath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel. <sup>39</sup>And his brother Asaph, who stood on his right hand, Asaph the son of Barachias, the son of Samaa. <sup>40</sup>The son of Michael, the son of Basaia, the son of Melchia.

translation.original_text: <sup>41</sup>filii Athanai, filii Zara, filii Adaia, <sup>42</sup>filii Ethan, filii Zamma, filii Semei, <sup>43</sup>filii Jeth, filii Gersom, filii Levi. <sup>44</sup>Filii autem Merari fratres eorum, ad sinistram, Ethan filius Cusi, filii Abdi, filii Maloch, <sup>45</sup>filii Hasabiæ, filii Amasiæ, filii Helciæ, <sup>46</sup>filii Amasai, filii Boni, filii Somer, <sup>47</sup>filii Moholi, filii Musi, filii Merari, filii Levi. <sup>48</sup>Fratres quoque eorum Levitæ, qui ordinati sunt in cunctum ministerium tabernaculi domus Domini. <sup>49</sup>Aaron vero et filii ejus adolebant incensum super altare holocausti, et super altare thymiamatis, in omne opus Sancti sanctorum : et ut precarentur pro Israël juxta omnia quæ præceperat Moyses servus Dei. <sup>50</sup>Hi sunt autem filii Aaron : Eleazar filius ejus, Phinees filius ejus, Abisue filius ejus,
translation.text: <sup>41</sup>The son of Athanai, the son of Zara, the son of Adaia. <sup>42</sup>The son of Ethan, the son of Zamma, the son of Semei. <sup>43</sup>The son of Jeth, the son of Gerson, the son of Levi. <sup>44</sup>And the sons of Merari their brethren, on the left hand, Ethan the son of Cusi, the son of Abdi, the son of Meloch, <sup>45</sup>The son of Hasabia, the son of Amasai, the son of Helcias, <sup>46</sup>The son of Amasai, the son of Boni, the son of Somer, <sup>47</sup>The son of Moholi, the son of Mud, the son of Merari, the son of Levi. <sup>48</sup>Their brethren also the Levites, who were appointed for all the ministry of the tabernacle of the house of the Lord. <sup>49</sup>But Aaron and his sons offered burnt offerings upon the altar of holocausts, and upon the altar of incense, for very work of the holy of holies: and to pray for Israel according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded. <sup>50</sup>And these are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinees his son, Abisue his son,

translation.original_text: <sup>51</sup>Bocci filius ejus, Ozi filius ejus, Zarahia filius ejus, <sup>52</sup>Meraioth filius ejus, Amarias filius ejus, Achitob filius ejus, <sup>53</sup>Sadoc filius ejus, Achimaas filius ejus. <sup>54</sup>Et hæc habitacula eorum per vicos atque confinia, filiorum scilicet Aaron, juxta cognationes Caathitarum : ipsis enim sorte contigerant. <sup>55</sup>Dederunt igitur eis Hebron in terra Juda, et suburbana ejus per circuitum : <sup>56</sup>agros autem civitatis, et villas, Caleb filio Jephone. <sup>57</sup>Porro filiis Aaron dederunt civitates ad confugiendum Hebron, et Lobna, et suburbana ejus, <sup>58</sup>Jether quoque, et Esthemo cum suburbanis suis, sed et Helon, et Dabir cum suburbanis suis, <sup>59</sup>Asan quoque, et Bethsemes, et suburbana earum. <sup>60</sup>De tribu autem Benjamin, Gabee et suburbana ejus, et Almath cum suburbanis suis, Anathoth quoque cum suburbanis suis : omnes civitates, tredecim, per cognationes suas.
translation.text: <sup>51</sup>Bocci his son, Ozi his son, Zarahia his son, <sup>52</sup>Meraioth his son, Amarias his son, Achitob his son, <sup>53</sup>Sadoc his son, Achimaas his son. <sup>54</sup>And these are their dwelling places by the towns and confines, to wit, of the sons of Aaron, of the families of the Caathites: for they fell to them by lot. <sup>55</sup>And they gave them Hebron in the land of Juda, and the suburbs thereof round about: <sup>56</sup>But the fields of the city, and the villages to Caleb son of Jephone. <sup>57</sup>And to the sons of Aaron they gave the cities for refuge Hebron, and Lobna, and the suburbs thereof, <sup>58</sup>And Jether and Esthemo, with their suburbs, and Helon, and Dabir with their suburbs: <sup>59</sup>Asan also, and Bethsames, with their suburbs. <sup>60</sup>And out of the tribe of Benjamin: Gabee and its suburbs, Almath with its suburbs, Anathoth also with its suburbs: all their cities throughout their families were thirteen.

translation.original_text: <sup>61</sup>Filiis autem Caath residuis de cognatione sua dederunt ex dimidia tribu Manasse in possessionem urbes decem. <sup>62</sup>Porro filiis Gersom per cognationes suas de tribu Issachar, et de tribu Aser, et de tribu Nephthali, et de tribu Manasse in Basan, urbes tredecim. <sup>63</sup>Filiis autem Merari per cognationes suas de tribu Ruben, et de tribu Gad, et de tribu Zabulon, dederunt sorte civitates duodecim. <sup>64</sup>Dederunt quoque filii Israël Levitis civitates, et suburbana earum : <sup>65</sup>dederuntque per sortem, ex tribu filiorum Juda, et ex tribu filiorum Simeon, et ex tribu filiorum Benjamin urbes has, quas vocaverunt nominibus suis, <sup>66</sup>et his, qui erant de cognatione filiorum Caath, fueruntque civitates in terminis eorum de tribu Ephraim. <sup>67</sup>Dederunt ergo eis urbes ad confugiendum, Sichem cum suburbanis suis in monte Ephraim, et Gazer cum suburbanis suis, <sup>68</sup>Jecmaam quoque cum suburbanis suis, et Bethoron similiter, <sup>69</sup>necnon et Helon cum suburbanis suis, et Gethremmon in eumdem modum. <sup>70</sup>Porro ex dimidia tribu Manasse, Aner et suburbana ejus, Balaam et suburbana ejus : his videlicet, qui de cognatione filiorum Caath reliqui erant.
translation.text: <sup>61</sup>And to the sons of Caath that remained of their kindred they gave out of the half tribe of Manasses ten cities in possession. <sup>62</sup>And to the sons of Gerson by their families out of the tribe of Issachar, and out of the tribe of Aser, and out of the tribe of Nephtali, and out of the tribe of Manasses in Basan, thirteen cities. their <sup>63</sup>And to the sons of Merari by families out of the tribe of Ruben, and out of the tribe of Gad, and out of the tribe of Zabulon, they gave by lot twelve cities. <sup>64</sup>And the children of Israel gave to the Levites the cities, and their suburbs. <sup>65</sup>And they gave them by lot, out of the tribe of the sons of Juda, and out of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, and out of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, these cities which they called by their names. <sup>66</sup>And to them that were of the kindred of the sons of Caath, and the cities in their borders were of the tribe of Ephraim. <sup>67</sup>And they gave the cities of refuge Sichem with its suburbs in mount Ephraim, and Gazer with its suburbs, <sup>68</sup>Jecmaan also with its suburbs, and Beth-horon in like manner, <sup>69</sup>Helon also with its suburbs, and Gethremmon in like manner, <sup>70</sup>And out of the half tribe of Manasses, Aner and its suburbs, Baalam and its suburbs: to wit, to them that were left of the family of the sons of Caath.

translation.original_text: <sup>71</sup>Filiis autem Gersom de cognatione dimidiæ tribus Manasse, Gaulon in Basan, et suburbana ejus, et Astaroth cum suburbanis suis. <sup>72</sup>De tribu Issachar, Cedes et suburbanis suis, et Dabereth cum suburbanis suis, <sup>73</sup>Ramoth quoque et suburbana ejus, et Anem cum suburbanis suis. <sup>74</sup>De tribu vero Aser : Masal cum suburbanis suis, et Abdon similiter, <sup>75</sup>Hucac quoque et suburbana ejus, et Rohob cum suburbanis suis. <sup>76</sup>Porro de tribu Nephthali, Cedes in Galilæa et suburbana ejus, Hamon cum suburbanis suis, et Cariathaim et suburbana ejus. <sup>77</sup>Filiis autem Merari residuis : de tribu Zabulon, Remmono et suburbana ejus, et Thabor cum suburbanis sus : <sup>78</sup>trans Jordanem quoque ex adverso Jericho contra orientem Jordanis, de tribu Ruben, Bosor in solitudine cum suburbanis suis, et Jassa cum suburbanis suis, <sup>79</sup>Cademoth quoque et suburbana ejus, et Mephaat cum suburbanis suis. <sup>80</sup>Necnon et de tribu Gad, Ramoth in Galaad et suburbana ejus, et Manaim cum suburbanis suis,
translation.text: <sup>71</sup>And to the sons of Gersom, out of the kindred of the half tribe of Manasses, Gaulon, in Basan, and its suburbs, and Astharoth with its suburbs. <sup>72</sup>Out of the tribe of Issachar, Cedes and its suburbs, and Dabereth with its suburbs; <sup>73</sup>Ramoth also and its suburbs, and Anem with its suburbs. <sup>74</sup>And out of the tribe of Aser: Masal with its suburbs, and Abdon in like manner; <sup>75</sup>Hucac also and its suburbs, and Rohol with its suburbs. <sup>76</sup>And out of the tribe of Nephtali, Cedes in Galilee and its suburbs, Hamon with its suburbs, and Cariathaim, and its suburbs. <sup>77</sup>And to the sons of Merari that remained: out of the tribe of Zabulon, Remmono and its suburbs, and Thabor with its suburbs. <sup>78</sup>Beyond the Jordan also over against Jericho, on the east side of the Jordan, out of the tribe of Ruben, Bosor in the wilderness with its suburbs, and Jassa with its suburbs; <sup>79</sup>Cademoth also and its suburbs, and Mephaath with its suburbs; <sup>80</sup>Moreover also out of the tribe of Gad, Ramoth in Galaad and its suburbs, and Manaim with its suburbs;

translation.original_text: <sup>81</sup>sed et Hesebon cum suburbanis suis, et Jezer cum suburbanis suis.
translation.text: <sup>81</sup>Hesebon also with its suburbs, and Jazer with its suburbs.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Porro filii Issachar : Thola, et Phua, Jasub, et Simeron, quatuor. <sup>2</sup>Filii Thola : Ozi, et Raphaia, et Jeriel, et Jemai, et Jebsem, et Samuel, principes per domos cognationum suarum. De stirpe Thola viri fortissimi numerati sunt in diebus David, viginti duo millia sexcenti. <sup>3</sup>Filii Ozi : Izrahia, de quo nati sunt Michaël, et Obadia, et Joël, et Jesia, quinque omnes principes. <sup>4</sup>Cumque eis per familias et populos suos, accincti ad prælium, viri fortissimi, triginta sex millia : multas enim habuerunt uxores, et filios. <sup>5</sup>Fratres quoque eorum per omnem cognationem Issachar robustissimi ad pugnandum, octoginta septem millia numerati sunt. <sup>6</sup>Filii Benjamin : Bela, et Bechor, et Jadihel, tres. <sup>7</sup>Filii Bela : Esbon, et Ozi, et Oziel, et Jerimoth, et Urai, quinque principes familiarum, et ad pugnandum robustissimi : numerus autem eorum, viginti duo millia et triginta quatuor. <sup>8</sup>Porro filii Bechor : Zamira, et Joas, et Eliezer, et Elioënai, et Amri, et Jerimoth, et Abia, et Anathoth, et Almath : omnes hi filii Bechor.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>Now the sons of Issachar were Thola, and Phua, Jasub and Simeron, four. <sup>2</sup>The sons of Thola: Ozi and Raphaia, and Jeriel, and Jemai, and Jebsem, and Samuel, chiefs of the houses of their kindreds. Of the posterity of Thola were numbered in the days of David, two and twenty thousand six hundred most valiant men. <sup>3</sup>The sons of Ozi: Izrahia, of whom were born Michael, and Obadia, and Joel, and Jesia, five all great men. <sup>4</sup>And there were with them by their families and peoples, six and thirty thousand most valiant men ready for war: for they had many wives and children. <sup>5</sup>Their brethren also throughout all the house of Issachar, were numbered fourscore and seven thousand most valiant men for war. <sup>6</sup>The sons of s Benjamin were Bela, and Bechor, and Jadihel, three. <sup>7</sup>The sons of Bela: Esbon, and Ozi, and Ozial, and Jerimoth and Urai, five chiefs of their families, and most valiant warriors, and their number was twenty-two thousand and thirty-four. <sup>8</sup>And the sons of Bechor were Zamira, and Joas, and Eliezer, and Elioenai, and Amai, and Jerimoth, and Abia, and Anathoth, and Almath: all these were the sons of Bechor.

translation.original_text: <sup>9</sup>Numerati sunt autem per familias suas principes cognationum suarum ad bella fortissimi, viginti millia et ducenti. <sup>10</sup>Porro filii Jadihel : Balan. Filii autem Balan : Jehus, et Benjamin, et Aod, et Chanana, et Zethan, et Tharsis, et Ahisahar : <sup>11</sup>omnes hi filii Jadihel, principes cognationum suarum viri fortissimi, decem et septem millia et ducenti ad prælium procedentes. <sup>12</sup>Sepham quoque et Hapham filii Hir : et Hasim filii Aher. <sup>13</sup>Filii autem Nephthali : Jaziel, et Guni, et Jeser, et Sellum, filii Bala. <sup>14</sup>Porro filius Manasse, Esriel : concubinaque ejus Syra peperit Machir patrem Galaad. <sup>15</sup>Machir autem accepit uxores filiis suis Happhim, et Saphan : et habuit sororem nomine Maacha : nomen autem secundi, Salphaad, natæque sunt Salphaad filiæ. <sup>16</sup>Et peperit Maacha uxor Machir filium, vocavitque nomen ejus Phares : porro nomen fratris ejus, Sares : et filii ejus, Ulam, et Recen.
translation.text: <sup>9</sup>And they were numbered by the families, heads of their kindreds, most valiant men for war, twenty thousand and two hundred. <sup>10</sup>And the son of Jadihel: Balan. And the sons of Balan: Jehus and Benjamin and Aod, and Chanana, and Zethan and Tharsis, and Ahisahar. <sup>11</sup>All these were sons of Jadihel, heads of their kindreds, most valiant men, seventeen thousand and two hundred fit to go out to war. <sup>12</sup>Sepham also and Hapham the sons of Hir: and Hasim the sons of Aher. <sup>13</sup>And the sons of Nephtali were Jasiel, and Guni, and Jezer, and Sellum, sons of Bala. <sup>14</sup>And the son of Manasses, Ezriel: and his concubine the Syrian bore Machir the father of Galaad. <sup>15</sup>And Machir took wives for his sons Happhim, and Saphan: and he had a sister named Maacha: the name of the second was Salphaad, and Salphaad had daughters. <sup>16</sup>And Maacha the wife of Machir bore a son, and she called his name Phares: and the name of his brother was Sares: and his sons were Ulam and Recen.

translation.original_text: <sup>17</sup>Filius autem Ulam, Badan : hi sunt filii Galaad, filii Machir, filii Manasse. <sup>18</sup>Soror autem ejus Regina peperit Virum decorum, et Abiezer, et Mohola. <sup>19</sup>Erant autem filii Semida, Ahin, et Sechem, et Leci, et Aniam. <sup>20</sup>Filii autem Ephraim : Suthala, Bared filius ejus, Thahath filius ejus, Elada filius ejus, Thahath filius ejus, hujus filius Zabad, <sup>21</sup>et hujus filius Suthula, et hujus filius Ezer et Elad : occiderunt autem eos viri Geth indigenæ, quia descenderant ut invaderent possessiones eorum. <sup>22</sup>Luxit igitur Ephraim pater eorum multis diebus, et venerunt fratres ejus ut consolarentur eum. <sup>23</sup>Ingressusque est ad uxorem suam : quæ concepit, et peperit filium, et vocavit nomen ejus Beria, eo quod in malis domus ejus ortus esset : <sup>24</sup>filia autem ejus fuit Sara, quæ ædificavit Bethoron inferiorem et superiorem, et Ozensara.
translation.text: <sup>17</sup>And the son of Ulam, Baden. These are the sons of Galaad, the son of Machir the son of Manasses. <sup>18</sup>And his sister named Queen bore Goodlyman, and Abiezer, and Mohola. <sup>19</sup>And the sons of Semida were Ahiu, and Sechem, and Leci and Aniam. <sup>20</sup>And the sons of Ephraim were Suthala, Bared his son, Thahath his son, Elada his son, Thahath his son, and his son Zabad, <sup>21</sup>And his son Suthala, and his son Ezer, and Elad: and the men of Geth born in the land slew them, because they came down to invade their possessions. <sup>22</sup>And Ephraim their father mourned many days, and his brethren came to comfort him. <sup>23</sup>And he went in to his wife: and she conceived and bore a son, and he called his name Beria, because he was born when it went evil with his house: <sup>24</sup>And his daughter was Sara, who built Bethoron, the nether and the upper, and Ozensara.

translation.original_text: <sup>25</sup>Porro filius ejus Rapha, et Reseph, et Thale, de quo natus est Thaan, <sup>26</sup>qui genuit Laadan : hujus quoque filius Ammiud, qui genuit Elisama, <sup>27</sup>de quo ortus est Nun, qui habuit filium Josue. <sup>28</sup>Possessio autem eorum et habitatio, Bethel cum filiabus suis, et contra orientem Noran, ad occidentalem plagam Gazer et filiæ ejus, Sichem quoque cum filiabus suis, usque ad Aza cum filiabus ejus. <sup>29</sup>Juxta filios quoque Manasse, Bethsan et filias ejus, Thanach et filias ejus, Mageddo et filias ejus, Dor et filias ejus : in his habitaverunt filii Joseph, filii Israël. <sup>30</sup>Filii Aser : Jemna, et Jesua, et Jessui, et Baria, et Sara soror eorum. <sup>31</sup>Filii autem Baria : Heber, et Melchiel : ipse est pater Barsaith. <sup>32</sup>Heber autem genuit Jephlat, et Somer, et Hotham, et Suaa sororem eorum.
translation.text: <sup>25</sup>And Rapha was his son, and Reseph, and Thale, of whom was born Thaan, <sup>26</sup>Who begot Laadan: and his son was Ammiud, who beget Elisama, <sup>27</sup>Of whom was born Nun, who had Josue for his son. <sup>28</sup>And their possessions and habitations were Bethel with her daughters, and eastward Noran, and westward Gazer and her daughters, Sichem also with her daughters, as far as Ass with her daughters. <sup>29</sup>And by the borders of the sons of Manasses Bethsan and her daughters, Thanach and her daughters, Mageddo and her daughters: Dor and her daughters: in these dwelt the children of Joseph, the son of Israel. <sup>30</sup>The children of Aser were Jemna, and Jesua, and Jessui, and Baria, and Sara their sister. <sup>31</sup>,31And the sons of Baria: Haber, and Melchiel: he is the father of Barsaith. <sup>32</sup>And Heber beget Jephlat, and Somer, and Hotham, and Suaa their sister.

translation.original_text: <sup>33</sup>Filii Jephlat : Phosech, et Chamaal, et Asoth : hi filii Jephlat. <sup>34</sup>Porro filii Somer : Ahi, et Roaga, et Haba, et Aram. <sup>35</sup>Filii autem Helem fratris ejus : Supha, et Jemna, et Selles, et Amal. <sup>36</sup>Filii Supha : Sue, Harnapher, et Sual, et Beri, et Jamra, <sup>37</sup>Bosor, et Hod, et Samma, et Salusa, et Jethran, et Bera. <sup>38</sup>Filii Jether : Jephone, et Phaspha, et Ara. <sup>39</sup>Filii autem Olla : Aree, et Haniel, et Resia. <sup>40</sup>Omnes hi filii Aser, principes cognationum, electi atque fortissimi duces ducum : numerus autem eorum ætatis quæ apta esset ad bellum, viginti sex millia.
translation.text: <sup>33</sup>The sons of Jephlat: Phosech, and Chamaal, and Asoth: these are the sons of Jephlat. <sup>34</sup>And the sons of Somer: Ahi, and Roaga, and Haba, and Aram. <sup>35</sup>And the sons of Helem his brother: Supha, and Jemna, and Selles, and Amal. <sup>36</sup>The sons of Supha: Sue, Hernapher, and Sual, and Beri, and Jamra. <sup>37</sup>Bosor and Hod, and Samma, and Salusa, and Jethran, and Bera. <sup>38</sup>The sons of Jether: Jephone, and Phaspha, and Ara. <sup>39</sup>And the sons of Olla: Aree, and Haniel, and Resia. <sup>40</sup>All these were sons of Aser, heads of their families, choice and most valiant captains of captains: and the number of them that were of the age that was fit for war, was six and twenty thousand.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Benjamin autem genuit Bale primogenitum suum, Asbel secundum, Ahara tertium, <sup>2</sup>Nohaa quartum, et Rapha quintum. <sup>3</sup>Fueruntque filii Bale : Addar, et Gera, et Abiud, <sup>4</sup>Abisue quoque et Naaman, et Ahoë, <sup>5</sup>sed et Gera, et Sephuphan, et Huram. <sup>6</sup>Hi sunt filii Ahod, principes cognationum habitantium in Gabaa, qui translati sunt in Manahath. <sup>7</sup>Naaman autem, et Achia, et Gera, ipse transtulit eos, et genuit Osa, et Ahiud. <sup>8</sup>Porro Saharaim genuit in regione Moab, postquam dimisit Husim et Bara uxores suas.
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>Now Benjamin beget Bale his firstborn, Asbel the second, Ahara the third, <sup>2</sup>Nohaa the fourth, and Rapha the fifth. <sup>3</sup>And the sons of Bale were Addar, and Gera, and Abiud, <sup>4</sup>And Abisue, and Naamar, and Ahoe, <sup>5</sup>And Gera, and Sephuphan, and Huram. <sup>6</sup>These are the sons of Ahod, heads of families that dwelt in Gabaa, who were removed into Mrtnahsth. <sup>7</sup>And Naaman, and Achia, and Gera he removed them, and beget Oza, and Ahiud. <sup>8</sup>And Saharim begot in the land of Moab, after he sent away Husim and Bara his wives.

translation.original_text: <sup>9</sup>Genuit autem de Hodes uxore sua Jobab, et Sebia, et Mosa, et Molchom, <sup>10</sup>Jehus quoque, et Sechia, et Marma : hi sunt filii ejus principes in familiis suis. <sup>11</sup>Mehusim vero genuit Abitob et Elphaal. <sup>12</sup>Porro filii Elphaal : Heber, et Misaam, et Samad : hic ædificavit Ono, et Lod, et filias ejus. <sup>13</sup>Baria autem et Sama principes cognationum habitantium in Ajalon : hi fugaverunt habitatores Geth. <sup>14</sup>Et Ahio, et Sesac, et Jerimoth, <sup>15</sup>et Zabadia, et Arod, et Heder, <sup>16</sup>Michaël quoque, et Jespha, et Joha filii Baria.
translation.text: <sup>9</sup>And he beget of Hodes his wife Jobab, and Sebia, and Mesa, and Molchom, <sup>10</sup>And Jehus and Sechia, and Marma. These were his sons heads of their families. <sup>11</sup>And Mehusim beget Abitob, and Elphaal. <sup>12</sup>And the sons of Elphaal were Heber, and Misaam, and Samad: who built One, and Led, and its daughters. <sup>13</sup>And Baria, and Sama were heads of their kindreds that dwelt in Aialon: these drove away the inhabitants of Geth. <sup>14</sup>And Ahio, and Sesac, and Jerimoth, <sup>15</sup>And Zabadia, and Arod, and Heder, <sup>16</sup>And Michael, and Jespha, and Joha, the sons of Baria.

translation.original_text: <sup>17</sup>Et Zabadia, et Mosollam, et Hezeci, et Heber, <sup>18</sup>et Jesamari, et Jezlia, et Jobab filii Elphaal, <sup>19</sup>et Jacim, et Zechri, et Zabdi, <sup>20</sup>et Elioënai, et Selethai, et Eliel, <sup>21</sup>et Adaia, et Baraia, et Samarath, filii Semei. <sup>22</sup>Et Jespham, et Heber, et Eliel, <sup>23</sup>et Abdon, et Zechri, et Hanan, <sup>24</sup>et Hanania, et Ælam, et Anathothia,
translation.text: <sup>17</sup>,17And Zabadia, and Mosollam, and Hezeci, and Heber, <sup>18</sup>And Jesamari, and Jezlia, and Jobab, sons of Elphaal, <sup>19</sup>And Jacim, and Zechri, and Zabdi, <sup>20</sup>And Elioenai, and Selethai, and Elial, <sup>21</sup>And Adaia, and Baraia, and Samareth, the sons of Semei. <sup>22</sup>And Jespham, and Heber, and Eliel, <sup>23</sup>And Abdon, and Zechri, and Hanan, <sup>24</sup>And Hanania, and Elam, and Anathothia.

translation.original_text: <sup>25</sup>et Jephdaia, et Phanuel, filii Sesac. <sup>26</sup>Et Samsari, et Sohoria, et Otholia, <sup>27</sup>et Jersia, et Elia, et Zechri, filii Jeroham. <sup>28</sup>Hi patriarchæ, et cognationum principes, qui habitaverunt in Jerusalem. <sup>29</sup>In Gabaon autem habitaverunt Abigabaon, et nomen uxoris ejus Maacha : <sup>30</sup>filiusque ejus primogenitus Abdon, et Sur, et Cis, et Baal, et Nadab, <sup>31</sup>Gedor quoque, et Ahio, et Zacher, et Macelloth : <sup>32</sup>et Macelloth genuit Samaa : habitaveruntque ex adverso fratrum suorum in Jerusalem cum fratribus suis.
translation.text: <sup>25</sup>And Jephdaia, and Phanuel the sons of Sesac. <sup>26</sup>And Samsari, and Sohoria and Otholia, <sup>27</sup>And Jersia, and Elia, and Zechri, the sons of Jeroham. <sup>28</sup>These were the chief fathers, and heads of their families who dwelt in Jerusalem. <sup>29</sup>And at Gabaon dwelt Abigabaon, and the name of his wife was Maacha: <sup>30</sup>And his firstborn son Abdon, and Sur, and Cia, and Baal, and Nadab, <sup>31</sup>And Gedor, and Ahio, and Zacher, and Macelloth: <sup>32</sup>And Macelloth beget Samaa: and they dwelt over against their brethren in Jerusalem with their brethren.

translation.original_text: <sup>33</sup>Ner autem genuit Cis, et Cis genuit Saul. Porro Saul genuit Jonathan, et Melchisua, et Abinadab, et Esbaal. <sup>34</sup>Filius autem Jonathan, Meribbaal : et Meribbaal genuit Micha. <sup>35</sup>Filii Micha, Phithon, et Melech, et Tharaa, et Ahaz. <sup>36</sup>Et Ahaz genuit Joada, et Joada genuit Alamath, et Azmoth, et Zamri : porro Zamri genuit Mosa, <sup>37</sup>et Mosa genuit Banaa, cujus filius fuit Rapha, de quo ortus est Elasa, qui genuit Asel. <sup>38</sup>Porro Asel sex filii fuerunt his nominibus : Ezricam, Bocru, Ismahel, Saria, Obdia, et Hanan : omnes hi filii Asel. <sup>39</sup>Filii autem Esec fratris ejus, Ulam primogenitus, et Jehus secundus, et Eliphalet tertius. <sup>40</sup>Fueruntque filii Ulam viri robustissimi, et magno robore tendentes arcum : et multos habentes filios ac nepotes, usque ad centum quinquaginta. Omnes hi filii Benjamin.
translation.text: <sup>33</sup>And Ner beget Cia, and Cia beget Saul. And Saul begot Jonathan and Melchisua, and Abinadab, and Esbaal. <sup>34</sup>And the son of Jonathan was Meribbaal: and Meribbaal begot Micha. <sup>35</sup>And the sons of Micha were Phithon, and Melech, and Tharaa, and Ahaz. <sup>36</sup>And Ahaz beget Joada: and Joada beget Alamath, and Azmoth, and Zamri: and Zamri beget Mesa, <sup>37</sup>And Mesa beget Banaa, whose son was Rapha, of whom was born Elasa, who beget Asel. <sup>38</sup>And Asel had six sons whose names were Ezricam, Bochru, Ismahel, Saria, Obdia, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Asel. <sup>39</sup>And the sons of Esec, his brother, were Ulam the firstborn, and Jehus the second, and Eliphelet the third. <sup>40</sup>And the sons of Ulam were most valiant men, and archers of great strength: and they had many sons and grandsons, even to a hundred and fifty. All these were children of Benjamin.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>Universus ergo Israël dinumeratus est, et summa eorum scripta est in libro regum Israël et Juda : translatique sunt in Babylonem propter delictum suum. <sup>2</sup>Qui autem habitaverunt primi in possessionibus et in urbibus suis : Israël, et sacerdotes, et Levitæ, et Nathinæi. <sup>3</sup>Commorati sunt in Jerusalem de filiis Juda, et de filiis Benjamin, de filiis quoque Ephraim, et Manasse. <sup>4</sup>Othei filius Ammiud, filii Amri, filii Omrai, filii Bonni, de filiis Phares filii Juda. <sup>5</sup>Et de Siloni : Asaia primogenitus, et filii ejus. <sup>6</sup>De filiis autem Zara, Jehuel, et fratres eorum, sexcenti nonaginta. <sup>7</sup>Porro de filiis Benjamin : Salo filius Mosollam, filii Oduia, filii Asana, <sup>8</sup>et Jobania filius Jeroham, et Ela filius Ozi, filii Mochori, et Mosollam filius Saphatiæ, filii Rahuel, filii Jebaniæ, <sup>9</sup>et fratres eorum per familias suas, nongenti quinquaginta sex. Omnes hi principes cognationum per domos patrum suorum. <sup>10</sup>De sacerdotibus autem : Jedaia, Jojarib, et Jachin :
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>And all Israel was numbered: and the sum of them was written in the book of the kings of Israel, and Juda: and they were carried away to Babylon for their transgression. <sup>2</sup>Now the first that dwelt in their possessions, and in their cities, were the Israelites, and the priests, and the Levites, and the Nathineans. <sup>3</sup>And in Jerusalem dwelt of the children of Juda, and of the children of Benjamin, and of the children of Ephraim, and of Manasses. <sup>4</sup>Othei the son of Ammiud, the son of Amri, the son of Omrai, the son of Bonni, of the sons of Phares the son of Juda. <sup>5</sup>And of Siloni: Asaia the firstborn, and his sons. <sup>6</sup>And of the sons of Zara: Jehuel, and their brethren, six hundred and ninety. <sup>7</sup>And of the sons of Benjamin: Sale the son of Mosollam, the son of Oduia, the son of Asana: <sup>8</sup>And Jobania the son of Jeroham: and Ela the son of Ozi, the son of Mochori: and Mosallam the son of Saphatias, the son of Rahuel, the son of Jebania: <sup>9</sup>And their brethren by their families, nine hundred and fifty-six. All these were heads of their families, by the houses of their fathers. <sup>10</sup>And of the priests: Jedaia, Joiarib, and Jachin:

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>Azarias quoque filius Helciæ, filii Mosollam, filii Sadoc, filii Maraioth, filii Achitob, pontifex domus Dei. <sup>12</sup>Porro Adaias filius Jeroham, filii Phassur, filii Melchiæ, et Maasai filius Adiel filii Jezra, filii Mosollam, filii Mosollamith, filii Emmer. <sup>13</sup>Fratres quoque eorum principes per familias suas, mille septingenti sexaginta, fortissimi robore ad faciendum opus ministerii in domo Dei. <sup>14</sup>De Levitis autem : Semeia filius Hassub filii Ezricam, filii Hasebia de filiis Merari. <sup>15</sup>Bacbacar quoque carpentarius, et Galal, et Mathania filius Micha, filii Zechri, filii Asaph : <sup>16</sup>et Obdia filius Semeiæ, filii Galal, filii Idithun : et Barachia filius Asa, filii Elcana, qui habitavit in atriis Netophati. <sup>17</sup>Janitores autem : Sellum, et Accub, et Telmon, et Ahimam : et frater eorum Sellum princeps, <sup>18</sup>usque ad illud tempus, in porta regis ad orientem, observabant per vices suas de filiis Levi. <sup>19</sup>Sellum vero filius Core filii Abiasaph, filii Core, cum fratribus suis, et domo patris sui, hi sunt Coritæ super opera ministerii, custodes vestibulorum tabernaculi : et familiæ eorum per vices castrorum Domini custodientes introitum. <sup>20</sup>Phinees autem filius Eleazari erat dux eorum coram Domino.
